sections.label: Section 1732
sections.translation: You are bringing up again the reproach of the Sphinx . Talk no more of past success . This misery was in store for you all the while ,

sections.label: Section 1735
sections.translation: to become an exile from your country and die anywhere .

sections.label: Section 1737
sections.translation: Leaving to my girlhood friends sad tears , I go forth from my native land , to roam as no maiden should .

sections.label: Section 1740
sections.translation: Ah ! This dutiful resolve towards my father ’ s suffering will make me famous . Alas for the insults heaped on you and on my brother , whose dead body goes from the house unburied ,

sections.label: Section 1745
sections.original: μέλεος , ὅν , εἴ με καὶ θανεῖν , πάτερ , χρεών ,
sections.translation: poor boy ! I will bury him secretly , though I have to die for it , father .

sections.label: Section 1750
sections.translation: They have enough of my piteous tale .

sections.label: Section 1751
sections.original: ἴθʼ ἀλλὰ Βρόμιος ἵνα τε σηκὸς
sections.translation: At least go seek the Bromian god in his untrodden sanctuary among the Maenads ’ hills .

sections.label: Section 1753
sections.translation: Bromius , for whom I once dressed in the Theban fawn-skin and

sections.label: Section 1755
sections.translation: danced upon the hills in the holy choir of Semele — shall I now offer the gods homage that is not homage ?

sections.label: Section 1758
sections.original: ὦ πάτρας κλεινῆς πολῖται , λεύσσετʼ , Οἰδίπους ὅδε ,
sections.translation: O citizens of a famous country , look at me ; I am Oedipus , who solved the famous riddle , and was the greatest of men ,

sections.label: Section 1760
sections.original: ὃς μόνος Σφιγγὸς κατέσχον τῆς μιαιφόνου κράτη ,
sections.translation: I , who alone controlled the murderous Sphinx ’ s power , am now myself driven from the land in dishonor and misery . But why do I make this moan and useless lamentation ? As a mortal , I must bear the constraint that the gods decree .

sections.label: Section 1764
sections.original: ὦ μέγα σεμνὴ Νίκη , τὸν ἐμὸν

sections.label: Section 1765
sections.translation: may you occupy my life and never cease to crown me !
